@@ -118,7 +118,7 @@
 {"id":"gt-1xsah","title":"Add gt role command group with env-based role detection","description":"# gt role Command Group

Make role detection less fragile by using env var injection instead of cwd detection.

## Env Vars
- `GT_ROLE` - authoritative role name (mayor, witness, refinery, polecat, crew)
- `GT_ROLE_HOME` - canonical home directory for the role

## Detection Precedence
1. `$GT_ROLE` env var (authoritative)
2. Fall back to cwd-based detection (legacy/manual)
3. Warn if they disagree

## Subcommands
- `gt role` / `gt role show` - show current role
- `gt role home [ROLE]` - show home dir for role
- `gt role detect` - force cwd-based detection (debugging)
- `gt role list` - list all known roles
- `gt role env` - print export statements

## Integration Points
- gt prime - show role, warn on mismatch
- gt mol status - use gt role instead of own detection
- gt handoff - preserve GT_ROLE, reset to home
- All spawners - inject GT_ROLE and GT_ROLE_HOME

## Background
Mayor wandered to refinery dir, handoff preserved it, role detection broke.
Env var injection makes role stable across cd operations.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-25T00:50:53.378051-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.539527-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.539527-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}

 {"id":"gt-1y0e","title":"🤝 HANDOFF: Investigate beads-sync divergence (221 files)","description":"## Context

gt doctor now detects orphaned code on beads-sync branch. Running it shows:

  beads-sync-orphans: 221 file(s) on beads-sync not in main

This is in the gastown repo (checking from crew/max).

## Background

- Earlier today we recovered orphaned mail migration code lost in merge 96c773f
- Recently simplified architecture: all workers use mayor/rig/.beads (redirects)
- This simplification may have caused beads-sync branch to diverge unexpectedly

## Investigation Steps

1. From crew/max run: git diff main..beads-sync --stat
2. Check if beads-sync should even exist anymore (was for multi-clone sync)
3. If obsolete with redirect architecture, consider deleting it
4. If it has legitimate changes, cherry-pick or merge to main

## Key Question

With all workers using mayor/rig/.beads via redirects, is beads-sync still needed?

## Commands

git log main..beads-sync --oneline | head -20
git diff main..beads-sync -- *.go | head -100","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","created_at":"2025-12-20T22:38:49.242099-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T22:38:49.242099-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-795e8","title":"Extract timing constants to constants package","description":"Extract hardcoded timing values to named constants.

## Files to modify
- internal/constants/constants.go (add constants)
- internal/cmd/start.go (use constants)
- internal/tmux/tmux.go (use constants)

## Implementation
Add to constants.go:
```go
const (
    ShutdownNotifyDelay  = 500 * time.Millisecond
    ClaudeStartTimeout   = 15 * time.Second
    ShellReadyTimeout    = 5 * time.Second
    DefaultDebounceMs    = 100
    DefaultDisplayMs     = 5000
)
```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] 5 timing constants added to constants.go
- [ ] start.go uses ShutdownNotifyDelay, ClaudeStartTimeout, ShellReadyTimeout
- [ ] tmux.go uses DefaultDebounceMs, DefaultDisplayMs
- [ ] go build ./... passes
- [ ] go test ./... passes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T15:49:12.251261-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/max","updated_at":"2025-12-28T17:17:52.768898-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T17:17:52.768898-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-79jxg","title":"Bug: bd update --parent doesn't persist","description":"The --parent flag on bd update reports success but doesn't persist the parent field.

**Confirmed behavior:**
```bash
bd update gt-9kp3t --parent=gt-d0jqp --no-daemon -v
# Output: ✓ Updated issue: gt-9kp3t

bd show gt-9kp3t --json | jq '.[0].parent'
# Returns: null

grep gt-9kp3t .beads/issues.jsonl | head -1 | jq '.parent'
# Returns: null (field not present)
```

**Root cause hypothesis:**
The update handler processes the --parent flag but either:
1. Doesn't write it to the Issue struct
2. Doesn't include it in the JSONL export
3. The field name doesn't match what export expects

**Files to check:**
- cmd/bd/show.go:638-640 (flag handling)
- cmd/bd/show.go:810-814 (reparenting logic)
- internal/rpc/server_issues_epics.go (RPC handler)
- internal/rpc/protocol.go (UpdateArgs.Parent field)

Related: bd-cj2e (the original feature implementation)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-27T22:40:33.744301-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-27T23:32:31.903138-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T23:32:31.903138-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-7asd","title":"Digest: mol-deacon-patrol","description":"Patrol 3: Routine","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T22:35:39.123943-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:26:04.650909-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:26:04.650909-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
-{"id":"gt-7aw1m","title":"Instrument gt commands to appear in activity feed","description":"## Architecture: Two-tier event system

```
┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐
│ gt commands │  │ bd mutations│  │ agent output│
└──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘
       └────────────────┼────────────────┘
                        ▼
              ~/gt/.events.jsonl (raw, audit)
                        │
              ┌─────────────────┐
              │  Feed daemon    │ ← goroutine in gt daemon
              │  - filters      │
              │  - aggregates   │
              │  - AI curates   │
              └────────┬────────┘
                       ▼
              ~/gt/.feed.jsonl (curated, user-facing)
```

## Event format

```json
{
  "ts": "2025-12-28T10:30:15Z",
  "source": "gt",
  "type": "sling",
  "actor": "mayor",
  "payload": {"bead": "gt-xyz", "target": "gastown/Toast"},
  "visibility": "feed"
}
```

Visibility: `audit` | `feed` | `both`

## Implementation phases

1. **Event logging** - gt commands write to ~/gt/.events.jsonl
2. **Feed daemon** - goroutine in gt daemon tails events, writes curated feed
3. **Feed readers** - gt feed, gt dashboard read from .feed.jsonl
4. **AI curation** - future: haiku summarization for complex activity

 {"id":"gt-gaxo","title":"ZFC Cleanup: Move Go heuristics to Deacon molecule","description":"Remove Go code that makes workflow decisions. All health checking, staleness 
detection, nudging, and escalation belongs in the Deacon molecule where Claude 
executes it once per minute.

## The Problem

Go code currently implements polling-based health inference:
- Daemon watches heartbeats with hardcoded thresholds
- Decides if agents are "stuck" based on timestamps  
- Sends nudges, triggers restarts
- Parses mail subjects with regex to extract intent

This is a ZFC violation. Go should be message transport, not decision-maker.

## The Fix

Move all health/oversight logic to Deacon patrol molecule:
- Deacon runs once per minute
- Claude reads molecule steps, executes them
- Claude decides if agents are stuck (by checking mail, activity, etc.)
- Claude sends nudges/escalations via mail
- Go just routes messages

## Scope

1. daemon/daemon.go - heartbeat staleness logic
2. daemon/backoff.go - exponential backoff decisions
3. daemon/lifecycle.go - regex parsing of lifecycle intent
4. keepalive/keepalive.go - staleness thresholds
5. deacon/heartbeat.go - heartbeat age classification
6. doctor/stale_check.go - staleness detection
7. polecat/manager.go - state derivation from issues

## Success Criteria

- Go code has no hardcoded time.Duration for health decisions
- No regex parsing of mail subjects for intent
- No switch statements deciding agent state
- Deacon molecule has all oversight logic
- Go daemon is just a message router","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:58:18.684884-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.754824-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.754824-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"epic"}
 {"id":"gt-gaxo.1","title":"Remove daemon heartbeat staleness logic","description":"**Files:** daemon/daemon.go (lines 272-325), daemon/backoff.go

**Current behavior:**
- Hardcoded 2min/5min thresholds classify agent health
- Exponential backoff decides nudge intervals
- Go decides "is this agent stuck?" based on timestamps

**Fix:**
- Remove staleness classification from Go
- Remove backoff algorithm
- Daemon becomes pure message router
- Deacon molecule step checks agent health instead

**Lines to remove/refactor:**
- daemon.go:272-274 (staleness thresholds)
- daemon.go:282-295 (poke decision logic)
- daemon.go:317-325 (conditional nudging)
- backoff.go:108-120 (backoff calculations)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:59:00.146652-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.7466-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gaxo.1","depends_on_id":"gt-gaxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:59:00.147277-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.7466-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-gaxo.2","title":"Remove keepalive/heartbeat staleness thresholds","description":"**Files:** 
- keepalive/keepalive.go (lines 94-111)
- deacon/heartbeat.go (lines 88-108)

**Current behavior:**
- Hardcoded thresholds: 2min=fresh, 5min=stale, 15min=very stale
- Used by daemon to decide whether to poke agents
- Go classifies agent responsiveness

**Fix:**
- Remove all hardcoded time.Duration thresholds
- Keepalive becomes pure timestamp storage
- Deacon molecule step does health assessment:
  "Check if Witness responded in last N minutes" (N from mol config)

**Lines to remove:**
- keepalive.go:96-110 (classification logic)
- heartbeat.go:91-107 (age classification)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:59:02.956489-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.738417-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gaxo.2","depends_on_id":"gt-gaxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:59:02.957013-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.738417-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-s18k","title":"Witness patrol: stale MR queue detection needs state tracking","description":"The mol-witness-patrol check-refinery step says 'if queue is non-empty for >30 min, send nudge' but doesn't explain how to track when the queue first became non-empty.

## Problem
The Witness needs to persist:
- When MR queue first became non-empty
- Previous queue state for comparison

Without this, the Witness can't detect stale queues.

## Solution
Add to witness handoff bead:
- mr_queue_nonempty_since: timestamp
- Update in save-state step
- Check in check-refinery step","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:02:38.497753-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:02:38.497753-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-s18k","depends_on_id":"gt-bjft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:12.026206-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-s3m0","title":"gt polecat: add 'done' or 'finish' command to transition from working to idle","description":"When a polecat finishes work but session wasn't properly cleaned up, there's no way to reset it from 'working' state back to 'idle'.

Tried:
```
gt polecat sleep gastown/Angharad
Error: sleeping polecat: polecat is not active (state: working)
```

The sleep command only works on 'active' polecats, not 'working' ones.

Had to manually edit state.json to reset:
```
jq '.state = "idle" | .issue = ""' state.json
```

Need a command like:
```
gt polecat done gastown/Angharad   # working -> idle
gt polecat finish gastown/Angharad # working -> idle  
gt polecat reset gastown/Angharad  # any state -> idle (force)
```","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:41:39.851037-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:57.018342-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:57.018342-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"gt-s6r44","title":"gt status: Show agent hook_bead (current work) in status output","description":"attached_args: Implement the hook_bead display in gt status. The agent bead lifecycle is complete - just need to read hook_bead and display it.

## Current State

gt status shows agents with their agent_state from beads:
```
   crew/max       ✓ running [running]
```

## Enhancement

Show what each agent is working on (hook_bead field from agent beads):
```
   crew/max       ✓ running [running] → gt-abc (Fix auth bug)
   crew/joe       ✓ running [running] → gt-xyz (Add tests)
   refinery       ✓ running → refinery Handoff
```

This gives the overseer a quick view of:
1. Who is running
2. What each agent is working on
3. Progress at a glance

## Implementation

In status.go, when displaying agents:
1. Look up agent bead by ID (e.g., gt-crew-gastown-max)
2. Parse hook_bead from description
3. If hook_bead set, fetch its title for display
4. Show as: agent_state → hook_bead (title truncated)

## Related

The agent bead lifecycle is now complete:
- prime.go creates agent beads
- sling.go sets hook_bead
- done.go clears hook_bead and sets final state","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-28T10:13:46.434234-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/max","updated_at":"2025-12-28T10:17:37.231773-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T10:17:37.231773-08:00"}
